title: Learning-Based Structural Decoding
author: Chawla, Aman
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This note constitutes the sixth step of the ten-step program for Quantum Structured Source Coding (QSSC). Steps 4 and 5 established a structured protocol for the atypical sector and proved that its active codebook of geometric labels can be truncated without large fidelity loss. The present step addresses the remaining operational question: how does a decoder identify the correct structural label from finite measurement data? We introduce a measurement-induced feature map obtained from a POVM applied to the received block, define nearest-centroid and margin-based structural decoders, and state explicit separation conditions under which these decoders recover the Step 5 label. Weak joint typicality is recovered as the special case in which the feature vector contains normalized empirical log-likelihood coordinates. We then prove a fidelity preservation theorem showing that the learning layer degrades the Step 5 recovery fidelity by at most the probability of structural misclassification. Finally, we distinguish search over the polynomially bounded structural label set from exponential full-codeword search and provide a worked diagonal-qubit example.
